summary: Two-story rectangular small building with brick walls, horizontal decorative bands, corner stone columns, a flat dark roof inset, and a raised white slab foundation/edge. Each floor has a row of evenly spaced windows and a wooden trim band between floors.

shape.overall_form: simple rectangular box with stepped perimeter foundation and flat recessed roof
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric on front/back and left/right with repeated window module

materials.name: wood_planks
materials.role: horizontal trim between floors / balcony ledge

elements: rows of small rectangular windows on each floor (5 visible on front face)
vertical corner columns (stone-like) spanning both floors
wooden trim band between floors and at base of each window row
white raised slab foundation / border around building
flat recessed dark roof (nether-brick fill) with light slab coping
rebuild_hints: Build a rectangular footprint approx 11x9 blocks. Raise a 1-block-high surrounding foundation border of quartz slab (white) inset from edge as shown.

Construct two full-height stories of brick walls, each story about 3 blocks tall; place a continuous wooden plank strip (1 block high) horizontally between stories and another at the base of the lower windows.
Place stone-like corner blocks (stonebrick or smooth stone) vertically at building corners spanning both floors to frame the brick walls.
Install evenly spaced single-block-high window openings (glass) across the front and sides: five modules across the front per floor as seen.
Top the structure with a recessed dark roofing fill (nether-brick) surrounded by a 1-block-high light-colored slab coping (stone slab or quartz slab).
uncertainties: Exact block types: some light blocks may be quartz or smooth stone variants; I labeled likely choices but exact material (quartz_slab vs stone_slab vs smooth_sandstone) is uncertain.
Dimensions are approximate: width (11) and depth (9) inferred from visible window/count pattern and aerial view; actual could differ by ±2 blocks.
Interior layout and presence/location of an entrance are not visible in provided views; entrance location is unknown.
The vertical corner blocks appear stone-like (stonebrick) but could be another grey textured block; confidence moderate.
